Off Calendar Oct-12-2005 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Harrison J. Frahn IV, appeared for Defendant/moving party
Steven A. Ellenberg, appeared for Plaintiff/Opposing party

Ruling:
Hearing held and Tentative Ruling adopted: Defendant Extinction Asset Management, LLC's Motion To Compel Further Responses To First Set of Requests For Documents, Continued Deposition Of Plaintiff, and Production of Documents Described In Deposition Notice is denied as it seeks tax returns and accounting records as it relates to those returns. There has been no waiver of the tax return privilege.
As the motion seeks additional financial records, the motion is denied as there is no good cause shown for the discovery of those records as set forth in this motion. Defendant, as the very least, must conduct further discovery to determine the evidence relied upon by Plaintiff in support of his claims regarding the valuation of the entity, supporting his claims that he had the financial wherewithall to buy EAM's interest in Bedrock or the claim that he could have made as much as $100 million from Bedrock.
There is no opposition to the continued deposition pursuant to the original notice of deposition as that deposition is limited to further questioning related to later produced documents pursuant to the original notice of deposition. The deposition is properly limited to that subject matter. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT

Re-Notice Of Motion & Motion To Quash, Or In The Alt., For Protective Order & To Stay Vince Pica Deposition; Compendium Of Non-Calif. Authorities
Off Calendar Jul-27-2005 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. DEFENDANT EXTINCTION ASSET MANAGEMENT, LLC'S Motion to quash deposition or in the alternative, for a protective order, denied. Defendant has not established any grounds under CCP 2025.420(b ) or CCP 1987.1 why this deposition should be quashed and no reason to delay it pending the motion for summary judgment. Defendant has failed to show that the burden or expense of taking this single deposition clearly outweighs the likelihood that the information sought will lead to the discovery of admissible evidence. Plaintiff has set forth specific reasons why he believes the discovery must be taken, and plaintiff is entitled to the opportunity to do so without delay. In light of the fact that the deposition was noticed in advance of the summary judgment motion filed by defendant, and given the pendency of the briefing on that motion, the parties are to meet and confer and schedule the deposition at the earliest mutually convenient date. Plaintiff's request for sanctions is denied. (622/M. MILLER)
